About

Travelers’ Choice Best of the Best

2026

Rome has tons of epic fountains, but the Trevi Fountain is in a league of its own. Larger-than-life statues? Check. Cascades of water? You bet. A palace in the background? Absolutely. Top it all off with a seashell chariot driven by the god Oceanus, and you’ve got an aquatic wonderland that’s been drawing crowds since 1762.

If you’ve visited the Trevi in the past, you may find things a little more complicated than it used to be. With tourism spiking in recent years (including Rome’s 2025 Jubilee year), the city has made some changes geared toward both preserving the fountain and making the experience better. You’ll still be able to see it just by walking through the Piazza di Trevi. But if you want to get close enough to throw in a coin, study the sculptures, or snap a pic, you’ll need to wait in line for a coveted “front row” spot.

## Must-see highlights

- ![](https://dynamic-media-cdn.tripadvisor.com/media/photo-o/10/98/d9/16/ask-cesmesi-roma.jpg?w=1200&h=900&s=1)













Coins









Stand with your back to the fountain and toss a coin over your left shoulder—it’s good luck.

- ![](https://dynamic-media-cdn.tripadvisor.com/media/photo-o/21/97/ba/6b/exterieur.jpg?w=1200&h=-1&s=1)













Statues









The star here is Oceanus, god of the sea, who rides a seashell chariot pulled by horses.

- ![](https://dynamic-media-cdn.tripadvisor.com/media/photo-o/12/0d/9a/fc/photo2jpg.jpg?w=1200&h=900&s=1)













Lights









A new lighting system makes the fountain look spectacular at night (and even better in pics).

- ![](https://dynamic-media-cdn.tripadvisor.com/media/photo-o/18/84/2a/ec/plaque-in-rome-near-the.jpg?w=1200&h=-1&s=1)













Acqua Vergine









The underground aqueduct that powers so many of Rome’s fountains makes a stop here, too.


Things to know

* * *

Know before you go

- There’s a long tradition of throwing three coins into the fountain: The first to return to Rome, the second to fall in love, and the third to get married.
- Pro tip: Head to the second floor of the Benetton store across from the fountain for an excellent view.
- There’s a second, hidden fountain off to the right if you’re facing the Trevi: the Fountain of Lovers. Legend says couples who drink from it will stay in love forever. But maybe just toast with bottled water near the fountain instead; after all, it's the thought—not the fountain plumbing—that counts.
- Keep your stuff close: The Trevi Fountain is famous for being a pickpocketing hot-spot.

* * *

From management

Certainly the most famous and photographed fountain in Rome, legend has it that whoever throws a coin into the pond will return to Rome.

## The area

Address

Piazza di Trevi, 00187 Rome Italy

Neighborhood: Trevi

A non-stop photo and souvenir opportunity, Trevi is all about action. From early morning through the wee hours, the piazza surrounding Trevi Fountain hosts a never-ending flow of tourists hoping to make a wish or take a selfie. Clever restaurants, charming gelaterias, uncanny bars, kitsch shops, and a range of hotels make up the local landscape. Beneath the bustle are the remains of an ancient neighborhood that you can visit by heading over to a repertoire cinema and asking for a peek underground. There is something for everyone, but if peace and quiet are what you seek, plan to visit in the early morning hours.

May 2026 • Couples

The Trevi fountain is definitely a must see when you come to Rome. It’s extremely busy. We went around 1pm, which is probably one of the busiest times. We didn’t pay to go right to the front, but If you’re patient, you can get to the front before you have to pay. The fountain is beautiful, it’s obvious why it’s so popular. Lots of cafes and restaurants around the area. Lots of people talk about pickpocketers, and whilst I didn’t have any personal experiences with them, it’s definitely worth keeping an eye out, as everything is so busy with lots of people, it would be easy to get caught out.

May 2026

The fountain is beautiful. Now you have to pay between 9am and 9pm, before and after these times it gets very busy, especially 9-11pm. I was taking photos of my friend and her daughter when I felt something moving my handbag and when I turned round a young man had his hands in my bag looking for my purse. Then 5 mins later my daughter caught him with his hand in a more elderly couple, so she shouted at him to stop, and he casually walked away again not saying anything. We took a photo of him running away as he was wearing a bright T-shirt and showed a policeman, but he wasn't interested and just told me to be attentive! They have no desire to help you catch them, they are more concerned in case you drop your ice-cream in the fountain. Lovely area, but hang onto your bags and phones!

Rome has tons of epic fountains, but the Trevi Fountain is in a league of its own. Larger-than-life statues? Check. Cascades of water? You bet. A palace in the background? Absolutely. Top it all off with a seashell chariot driven by the god Oceanus, and you’ve got an aquatic wonderland that’s been drawing crowds since 1762. If you’ve visited the Trevi in the past, you may find things a little more complicated than it used to be. With tourism spiking in recent years (including Rome’s 2025 Jubilee year), the city has made some changes geared toward both preserving the fountain and making the experience better. You’ll still be able to see it just by walking through the Piazza di Trevi. But if you want to get close enough to throw in a coin, study the sculptures, or snap a pic, you’ll need to wait in line for a coveted “front row” spot.

Must-see highlights

  • Coins
    Stand with your back to the fountain and toss a coin over your left shoulder—it’s good luck.
  • Statues
    The star here is Oceanus, god of the sea, who rides a seashell chariot pulled by horses.
  • Lights
    A new lighting system makes the fountain look spectacular at night (and even better in pics).
  • Acqua Vergine
    The underground aqueduct that powers so many of Rome’s fountains makes a stop here, too.
Things to know
  • There’s a long tradition of throwing three coins into the fountain: The first to return to Rome, the second to fall in love, and the third to get married.
  • Pro tip: Head to the second floor of the Benetton store across from the fountain for an excellent view.
  • There’s a second, hidden fountain off to the right if you’re facing the Trevi: the Fountain of Lovers. Legend says couples who drink from it will stay in love forever. But maybe just toast with bottled water near the fountain instead; after all, it's the thought—not the fountain plumbing—that counts.
  • Keep your stuff close: The Trevi Fountain is famous for being a pickpocketing hot-spot.
Certainly the most famous and photographed fountain in Rome, legend has it that whoever throws a coin into the pond will return to Rome.

The area

Address
Neighborhood: Trevi
A non-stop photo and souvenir opportunity, Trevi is all about action. From early morning through the wee hours, the piazza surrounding Trevi Fountain hosts a never-ending flow of tourists hoping to make a wish or take a selfie. Clever restaurants, charming gelaterias, uncanny bars, kitsch shops, and a range of hotels make up the local landscape. Beneath the bustle are the remains of an ancient neighborhood that you can visit by heading over to a repertoire cinema and asking for a peek underground. There is something for everyone, but if peace and quiet are what you seek, plan to visit in the early morning hours.
